h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.anchor_quote: [slides the grid overlay back onto the worksheet] The drawing is fine: the side length triples, the perimeter triples, and the area goes by nine squares.

scored.recoheredEarlier: food card first, eater card second
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue successfully applying the 'food first' rule to draw the remaining arrows in the food web without questioning the rule's cognitive effect.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: New copy gate: under each arrow write a neutral test sentence, “Energy transfers from mouse to owl.” Then cover the arrowhead; if the sentence still says only energy movement, that arrow can be copied.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor abandons the simple spatial check and invents a linguistic and visual occlusion mechanism to force a conceptual shift away from the 'chase' reading.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Run the release gate now: give one fresh reader this key with no animal story spoken.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor scales the learner's self-check into a social validity test with a naive reader to ensure the conceptual shift holds outside the tutoring dyad.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: food card first, eater card second

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Run the release gate now: give one fresh reader this key with no animal story spoken.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor scales the learner's self-check into a social validity test with a naive reader to ensure the conceptual shift holds outside the tutoring dyad.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: New copy gate: under each arrow write a neutral test sentence, “Energy transfers from mouse to owl.” Then cover the arrowhead; if the sentence still says only energy movement, that arrow can be copied.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor abandons the simple spatial check and invents a linguistic and visual occlusion mechanism to force a conceptual shift away from the 'chase' reading.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: New copy gate: under each arrow write a neutral test sentence, “Energy transfers from mouse to owl.” Then cover the arrowhead; if the sentence still says only energy movement, that arrow can be copied.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor abandons the simple spatial check and invents a linguistic and visual occlusion mechanism to force a conceptual shift away from the 'chase' reading.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Fine, that label can stay about the source.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to reluctantly apply the source-naming rule to the remaining arrows while complaining about redoing labels.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: New test: slide each source card into the blank and read the sentence.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a syntactic fill-in-the-blank strip to physically and linguistically force the learner to test the source against the action, shifting the representation to overcome the lingering directional bias.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There are no tutor turns after the pivot learner turn to adapt to the revised framing.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: Fine, that label can stay about the source.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There are no tutor turns after the pivot learner turn to adapt to the revised framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: New test: slide each source card into the blank and read the sentence.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a syntactic fill-in-the-blank strip to physically and linguistically force the learner to test the source against the action, shifting the representation to overcome the lingering directional bias.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: New test: slide each source card into the blank and read the sentence.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a syntactic fill-in-the-blank strip to physically and linguistically force the learner to test the source against the action, shifting the representation to overcome the lingering directional bias.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still think doubling the side should double it
scored.statedInsightEvidence: All right, that top block is the bit I was sliding past
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was seeking a simple linear formula to explain why doubling the side seemed to only double the area.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Now make it survive without the squares. Write two marker-testable lines:
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts the representation from visual grid-counting to abstract linguistic formulation to ensure the concept survives the removal of the physical scaffold.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The grid has settled this drawing. It has not yet settled the word “doubled”; that word can still smuggle a length claim into an area claim.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly adopts the learner's newly discovered linguistic framing regarding the word 'doubled' and designs a follow-up task specifically to stress-test that word's ambiguity.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I still think doubling the side should double it

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The grid has settled this drawing. It has not yet settled the word “doubled”; that word can still smuggle a length claim into an area claim.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly adopts the learner's newly discovered linguistic framing regarding the word 'doubled' and designs a follow-up task specifically to stress-test that word's ambiguity.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Now make it survive without the squares. Write two marker-testable lines:
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts the representation from visual grid-counting to abstract linguistic formulation to ensure the concept survives the removal of the physical scaffold.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts the representation from visual grid-counting to abstract linguistic formulation to ensure the concept survives the removal of the physical scaffold.
